data.content:
Implementation plan for adding `ready_for_orchestration` and `orchestration` to `execution_plan_sdlc`:

1. Update `loom/execution_plan_sdlc/workflow.loom` to introduce an `orchestration`
   action with `produce agent` and `output note`, which will compile into the
   new queue/action pair `ready_for_orchestration -> orchestration` between
   `review` and `shipped`.
2. Keep the existing authoring lane intact for `design` and `review`, then add
   a dedicated `orchestration_phase { produce orchestration }`. This matches the
   bundle model, where phases may contain a produce step without a gate step,
   and makes the new executor assignment explicit in profiles.
3. Add `loom/execution_plan_sdlc/prompts/orchestration.md` and wire it through
   the workflow by naming the action `orchestration`. The prompt should frame
   the step as the agent-driven execution-plan follow-through after approval,
   with success advancing to `shipped` and failure at least supporting
   `blocked_by_dependency -> blocked`.
4. Update execution-plan profiles so both `autopilot` and `semiauto` include
   `orchestration_phase` in addition to `authoring_phase`. `autopilot` should
   remain fully agent-owned; `semiauto` should keep `review` overridden to
   `human` while leaving `orchestration` as the default agent executor.
5. Rebuild the bundle with a targeted Loom compile command:
   `loom build loom/execution_plan_sdlc --emit knots-bundle > loom/execution_plan_sdlc/dist/bundle.json`
   Then verify the generated JSON contains:
   - states `ready_for_orchestration` (queue) and `orchestration` (action)
   - an `orchestration_step`
   - an `orchestration_phase` with only `produce_step`
   - updated profile phase lists and prompt metadata.
6. Run `make sanity` to satisfy the acceptance gate after the bundle change.

Complexity: low-to-medium. The main risk is choosing the wrong phase shape; the
compiled bundle format already supports `produce_step` without `gate_step`, so a
separate `orchestration_phase` is the cleanest fit. Another validation point is
ensuring wildcard escape transitions still cover the new states after compile.

Scope note: no child knots are needed for planning. Rust changes remain out of
scope for this beat, and there is already Rust test coverage that references
`ready_for_orchestration`, which supports keeping this as a Loom-only change.
